title: Ancient African Canoe Surfaces
story: Buried for thousands of years, a remarkable discovery surfaced in 1987. Archaeologists unearthed the Dufuna Canoe in northeastern Nigeria on May 4. This wooden vessel, between 8,000 and 8,500 years old, is the oldest known boat in Africa. Its discovery reveals the impressive boat-building skills of ancient African civilizations and provides a fascinating glimpse into their way of life along rivers and lakes long ago.

title: Mighty Itaipu Dam Powers Up
story: Electricity crackled to life as the massive Itaipu Dam began generating power in 1984. Straddling the border of Paraguay and Brazil, this engineering marvel harnessed the mighty Paraná River. On May 4, its turbines whirred into action, creating a surge of clean energy. Today, Itaipu stands as the world's second-largest electricity generator, powering millions of homes and businesses across two nations.

title: A Marble Library of Buddhist Wisdom
story: Imagine a book so big you could walk through it! In 1868, on May 4, the world's largest book opened in Mandalay, Burma. Called the Kuthodaw Inscription Shrines, it's not a regular book. Instead, it's 729 marble tablets carved with the entire Buddhist scriptures. Each tablet sits in its own small building, creating a unique outdoor library of stone pages.

Gordone's work would go on to earn him widespread acclaim and a special place in the annals of American theater on May 4.
activity_question: What was the name of Charles Gordone's play that premiered in 1969?
follow_up_question: Why do you think plays about identity are important?

title: Ice Hockey Star Skates into History
story: Gretchen Ulion glided onto the ice and into sports history. Born in Marlborough, Connecticut on May 4, 1972, she became a powerhouse in women's ice hockey. Ulion's talent shone at Dartmouth College, where she was named Ivy League Player of the Year twice. But her biggest triumph came in 1998 when she helped the U.S. women's ice hockey team win their first-ever Olympic gold medal.
activity_question: What major achievement did Gretchen Ulion accomplish in 1998?
follow_up_question: What college did Gretchen Ulion attend?

title: Soul Queen Sharon Jones Enters the World
story: A powerful voice was born in Augusta, Georgia in 1956. Sharon Jones, future queen of soul music, arrived on May 4. She would grow up to lead the band Sharon Jones & The Dap-Kings, thrilling audiences with her dynamic performances. Jones brought old-school soul back into the spotlight, inspiring a new generation of music lovers.
activity_question: Where was Sharon Jones born?
bonus_fact: Sharon Jones worked as a corrections officer before becoming a full-time singer at age 40.

title: Renee Powell: Trailblazing Golfer
story: Renee Powell swung into history in 1946. Born on May 4 in East Canton, Ohio, she grew up to become a groundbreaking golfer. Powell shattered barriers by competing in a men's golf tournament - a first for women. Her talent extended beyond the greens as she later became a respected broadcaster, sharing her love and knowledge of the sport with fans everywhere.
activity_question: What other career did Renee Powell pursue besides golf?
follow_up_question: How do you think Renee Powell's achievements inspired other female athletes?
